evals.prompt: I have a project with a Postgres database, a Python FastAPI backend in ./api, and a React frontend in ./web. The backend needs DATABASE_URL set and the frontend needs VITE_API_URL. Can you write me an agent-procs.yaml and then start everything up? The backend listens on port 8000 and the frontend on 5173.
evals.expected_output: A valid agent-procs.yaml with correct dependencies (db -> api -> frontend), ready patterns, env vars, and cwd fields. Then commands to start and verify the stack.

assertions.description: YAML defines db, api, and frontend processes
api depends_on db; frontend depends_on api
api has DATABASE_URL, frontend has VITE_API_URL
Each process has a ready field
api has cwd ./api, frontend has cwd ./web
Commands include agent-procs status after startup

evals.prompt: I need to start my Rails server in the background so I can keep working on code. It takes about 10 seconds to boot and prints 'Listening on http://0.0.0.0:3000' when ready. After it's up I want to run the integration test suite against it, and then shut everything down.
evals.expected_output: Uses agent-procs run with --name, agent-procs wait with --until and --timeout, runs the tests, then agent-procs stop.

assertions.description: Uses agent-procs run to start the server, not nohup or &
Uses --name flag to name the server process
Uses agent-procs wait with --until to detect readiness
Wait command includes --timeout with a reasonable value (>= 10s)
Integration tests execute after the wait command
Uses agent-procs stop or stop-all to shut down after tests

evals.prompt: I've got three microservices I need running for local dev: a gateway (port 4000), users-service (port 4001), and orders-service (port 4002). They're all in separate directories under ./services/. The gateway depends on both other services. Each one prints 'Server started' when ready. Can you set this up so I can just run one command to start everything?
evals.expected_output: Creates an agent-procs.yaml with all three services, correct dependency graph (gateway depends on users and orders), ready patterns, cwd fields. Uses agent-procs up to start everything.

assertions.description: YAML defines gateway, users-service, and orders-service
Gateway's depends_on includes both other services
users-service and orders-service do not depend on each other
Uses agent-procs up to start everything
